raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.3,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Making a research abstract 'stronger' centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim; the artifact currently undersells the clinical significance of the bottleneck and states the contribution in hedged, self-deprecating terms ('our contribution lies in showing that…').",
    "R1.4": "The novelty claim ('first application of multi-sample reasoning aggregation to clinical variant interpretation') is asserted but not substantiated or bounded; strengthening requires clearer delta positioning against nearest prior work such as self-consistency prompting, existing ensemble VUS classifiers, and clinical NLP systems.",
    "R1.6": "Prose-level improvements — sentence rhythm, precision of terminology, rhetorical momentum — are a near-certain component of any 'make stronger' request applied to scientific writing and are load-bearing here."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should 'make this stronger' prioritize (A) sentence-level prose improvements — rhetorical impact, word choice, pacing — while preserving the existing conceptual structure and claims; or (B) conceptual and argumentative improvements — restructuring or elevating claims, better situating novelty against prior work, tightening the logical chain from evidence to conclusions?"
}

rationale.R1.1: Making a research abstract 'stronger' centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim; the artifact currently undersells the clinical significance of the bottleneck and states the contribution in hedged, self-deprecating terms ('our contribution lies in showing that…').
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim ('first application of multi-sample reasoning aggregation to clinical variant interpretation') is asserted but not substantiated or bounded; strengthening requires clearer delta positioning against nearest prior work such as self-consistency prompting, existing ensemble VUS classifiers, and clinical NLP systems.
rationale.R1.6: Prose-level improvements — sentence rhythm, precision of terminology, rhetorical momentum — are a near-certain component of any 'make stronger' request applied to scientific writing and are load-bearing here.

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'make this stronger' prioritize (A) sentence-level prose improvements — rhetorical impact, word choice, pacing — while preserving the existing conceptual structure and claims; or (B) conceptual and argumentative improvements — restructuring or elevating claims, better situating novelty against prior work, tightening the logical chain from evidence to conclusions?
